The skills are standardized across institutions but deployment requires substantial customization, making this T2.

title: Medical records specialists
exposure_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the processing, classification, and entry of data into electronic systems. AI is already highly capable of natural language processing and automated medical coding, which directly automates the core tasks of reviewing records and assigning clinical codes, significantly reducing the need for human intervention.
tier_rationale: Medical records work requires deep integration with the enterprise's specific EHR system (Epic, Cerner, etc.), billing systems, and coding workflows, plus tuning to the facility's documentation patterns and payer rules. The skills are standardized (ICD-10, CPT coding) but an agent like Abridge or similar needs per-hospital integration, compliance/HIPAA configuration, and escalation logic to clinicians. Same agent product, but every deployment requires substantial integration work.

title: Medical scientists
exposure_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.
tier_rationale: Medical scientists do frontier research requiring deep tacit expertise — hypothesis generation, experimental design intuition, and interpretation of ambiguous results rely on judgment that even expert scientists cannot fully articulate. While portions (literature review, statistical analysis, grant drafting) are genericizable, the core scientific judgment requires the expert themselves to harness-engineer an agent through close iteration. Lab-specific protocols and research directions vary too idiosyncratically for a horizontal or framework-based agent product.

title: Medical transcriptionists
exposure_rationale: Medical transcription is a purely digital, routine information-processing task that has already been heavily impacted by speech recognition. Modern AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) can now handle complex medical terminology, formatting, and summarization with high accuracy, transforming the human role into a diminishing oversight function or eliminating it entirely.
tier_rationale: An AI agent for medical transcription (like Abridge) needs deep integration with the specific hospital's EHR system, custom templates for different physician specialties and document types, and tuning to local terminology and workflows. The core skill is generic but enterprise deployment requires substantial per-customer integration work into clinical systems with HIPAA compliance.

title: Music directors and composers
exposure_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital work (composing) and lower-exposure physical work (conducting). Generative AI is rapidly automating music composition, arrangement, and scoring for commercial media, while the music director role remains more resilient due to the need for live leadership, physical rehearsals, and human-to-human artistic interpretation.
tier_rationale: Music direction and composition are creative/performative knowledge work where value comes from artistic taste, interpretive vision, and ensemble leadership — tacit qualities that even the artist can't fully articulate. An agent could assist with arrangement drafting or score prep, but the core artistic judgment must be iteratively shaped by the individual composer/director. There's no enterprise integration story; deployment would be per-artist harness work.

title: Natural sciences managers
exposure_rationale: This role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving data analysis, budgeting, and technical reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While the job requires significant human-centric leadership and physical oversight of laboratories, AI will drastically increase productivity in reviewing research, drafting operational reports, and optimizing resource allocation.
tier_rationale: Natural sciences managers lead research teams, hire and evaluate scientists, secure budgets, and communicate with executives, clients, and policymakers — value lives in relationships, leadership, and accountability for high-stakes scientific decisions. Portable work (drafting reports, reviewing literature, scheduling) can be augmented by horizontal agents, but the managerial core depends on trust, organizational standing, and judgment that cannot be transferred to an agent. Some tacit scientific judgment also factors in, but the dominant constraint is relational/leadership authority.

title: Network and computer systems administrators
exposure_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and involves tasks like configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and 'infrastructure as code' practices. While some physical hardware maintenance remains, the shift toward cloud computing and AI-powered network management tools is already leading to a projected decline in employment as fewer workers can manage larger, more complex systems.
tier_rationale: A sysadmin AI agent requires deep integration into each enterprise's specific network topology, server inventory, identity providers, monitoring tools, ticketing systems, and security policies. The core skills (Linux/Windows admin, networking, troubleshooting) are generic, but deploying an agent to actually manage an organization's infrastructure requires substantial per-customer configuration, credentialed access, and tuning to local conventions and escalation paths.

title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
exposure_rationale: The core work product is digital (text, scripts, and multimedia), and AI can already automate significant portions of research, drafting, and editing. While physical presence for field reporting and the cultivation of human sources provide a protective barrier, the overall occupation faces high exposure as AI tools drastically increase individual productivity, potentially leading to further industry consolidation and fewer required roles.
tier_rationale: Journalism's core value lies in cultivated source relationships, on-the-ground interviewing, and trust-based access to people willing to share tips and leads—these relational assets stay with the human reporter. An AI agent can substantially augment portable parts (research, drafting, editing, transcription, multimedia production) but cannot replace the human who has cultivated the contacts and conducts in-person interviews. The work is also partially tacit (editorial judgment, news instinct), but the binding constraint is relational.

title: Nuclear engineers
exposure_rationale: Nuclear engineering is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—such as reactor design, dose calculations, and safety simulations—are performed using digital tools and complex mathematical modeling. While the high-stakes regulatory environment and the need for physical inspections at power plants provide a buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in its ability to optimize designs, analyze sensor data for predictive maintenance, and automate technical documentation.
tier_rationale: Nuclear engineering involves deep tacit expertise around reactor physics, safety judgment, and regulatory compliance specific to particular facilities and equipment, where mistakes are catastrophic and judgment is hard to fully articulate. An agent would need to be built by the experts themselves through close iteration on plant-specific systems, with senior engineers in the loop validating safety-critical decisions. Heavy regulatory and physical-plant context makes this resistant to either horizontal or framework-based deployment.

title: Operations research analysts
exposure_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data collection, mathematical modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI and Large Language Models excel. While analysts still provide high-level human judgment and stakeholder persuasion, AI can now automate the core technical tasks of coding analytical tools, mining data, and generating optimization simulations, significantly increasing individual productivity and restructuring the role.
tier_rationale: OR analysts solve enterprise-specific optimization problems requiring deep integration with company databases, ERP/supply chain systems, and proprietary business processes. The analytical methods (forecasting, simulation, optimization) are generic, but agent deployment needs per-enterprise data connectors, domain context, and tuned outputs trusted by managers — fitting the T2 pattern of generic framework with substantial custom configuration.

title: Paralegals and legal assistants
exposure_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—legal research, document drafting, and information organization—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models. While some interpersonal and courtroom logistics remain, AI can already automate or significantly accelerate the vast majority of a paralegal's workload, leading to the BLS's projection of 0% job growth despite high demand for legal services.
tier_rationale: A paralegal AI agent (like Harvey) needs deep per-firm integration with document management systems (iManage, NetDocuments), case management tools, court e-filing systems, and firm-specific templates and workflows. While the underlying skills (legal research, drafting) are similar across firms, each deployment requires substantial configuration to firm precedents, practice area conventions, and matter files. Same product, custom configuration per enterprise.

title: Personal financial advisors
exposure_rationale: The core technical tasks of this role—analyzing market data, optimizing portfolios, and tax planning—are digital and highly susceptible to AI automation, as evidenced by the rise of robo-advisors. However, the occupation is protected by a significant interpersonal component, as clients often require human trust, emotional reassurance during market volatility, and complex ethical judgment that AI cannot yet fully replicate.
tier_rationale: Personal financial advisors' value is fundamentally relational — clients choose advisors based on trust built over years, and the work involves making clients 'feel comfortable' with their financial decisions and life circumstances. An AI agent can augment the portable analytical work (portfolio analysis, research, plan drafting, monitoring) but the relational core of client meetings, trust-building, and sales/networking stays with the human advisor.

title: Physicists and astronomers
exposure_rationale: Physicists and astronomers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including data analysis, mathematical modeling, and software development. While AI significantly accelerates data processing and literature review, the core requirements of original theoretical innovation, experimental design, and complex physical-world problem solving provide a buffer against full automation.
tier_rationale: Frontier physics and astronomy research depends on deeply tacit expert intuition about which theories to pursue, how to design experiments, and how to interpret anomalous results — knowledge that even leading researchers cannot fully articulate. An agent for this work must be harness-engineered by the scientist themselves, iterating closely on their specific research program rather than deployed as a generic or enterprise-configured product.

title: Political scientists
exposure_rationale: Political science is a purely digital knowledge-work occupation centered on data analysis, research, and writing, all of which are core strengths of generative AI and LLMs. While high-level strategic judgment and interpersonal networking remain human-centric, AI can now automate the collection of public opinion data, the drafting of policy briefs, and the forecasting of political trends, leading to significant productivity gains and a projected decline in total employment.
tier_rationale: Political scientists do research work—gathering public data, analyzing policies, writing reports—using standard tools (documents, public datasets, survey data). A horizontal research/analysis agent with access to the researcher's files and public sources can do this work without per-enterprise integration. The outputs (reports, analyses, forecasts) are standard across employers.

title: Postsecondary education administrators
exposure_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on digital information processing, including reviewing applications, analyzing student data, managing budgets, and maintaining academic records—all tasks where AI can significantly increase productivity or automate routine workflows. While roles like deans and student affairs officers require high-level human judgment and interpersonal relationship management, the underlying administrative and analytical work is highly susceptible to AI integration and restructuring.
tier_rationale: Postsecondary administrators (deans, provosts, admissions officers, student affairs) derive most of their value from relationships with faculty, students, parents, and external stakeholders, plus institution-specific governance and politics. An agent could automate portable pieces (application screening, registrar data processing, scheduling) but the core advising, judgment calls on admissions/tenure, and stakeholder management stay with humans. The relational and institutional-political core dominates, making this T3c.

title: Postsecondary teachers
exposure_rationale: Postsecondary teaching is primarily knowledge work involving research, writing, and curriculum design—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human interaction and mentorship, AI can automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews, potentially increasing individual productivity to the point of restructuring faculty needs.
tier_rationale: Postsecondary teaching combines relational mentorship (advising students, dissertation supervision, committee work, building scholarly networks) with genuinely tacit research expertise in a specialized subfield. While portable tasks like syllabus drafting, lecture prep, and grading could be handled by a horizontal agent, the core value—mentoring specific students, peer-reviewed original research, and institutional governance—depends on established relationships and credentialed standing that stay with the human.

title: Producers and directors
exposure_rationale: Producers and directors are highly exposed because their core work products—scripts, visual aesthetics, and edited video—are increasingly being generated or enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires significant human leadership and physical presence on set, the rapid advancement of generative AI in pre-production (scripting/storyboarding) and post-production (editing/VFX) allows a single director to do the work of a much larger team, potentially consolidating the industry.
tier_rationale: Producers and directors combine relational work (assembling cast/crew, raising money, managing talent relationships) with deeply tacit creative judgment (interpreting scripts, shaping performance, defining a production's look and feel). The relational and reputational core — who you know, who trusts your vision, who will finance and act for you — cannot be transferred to an agent, though agents can help with scheduling, budgeting, research, and drafting. T3c fits best given the centrality of relationships with talent, financiers, and crew, with tacit creative gestalt as a secondary T3b element.

title: Project management specialists
exposure_rationale: Project management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation involving data analysis, scheduling, and documentation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While AI can significantly increase productivity in planning and monitoring, the role's heavy reliance on human-centric skills like stakeholder negotiation, conflict resolution, and team leadership provides a protective barrier against full automation.
tier_rationale: Project management is fundamentally relational and coordinative — value comes from established trust with clients, stakeholder management, navigating internal politics, and resolving ambiguity through human conversations. An agent could automate the portable artifacts (Gantt charts, status reports, budget tracking, risk registers) but the core work of aligning people, negotiating scope changes, and being the trusted point of contact stays with the human. Tools and integrations vary heavily (Jira, MS Project, Primavera, SAP) but the binding constraint isn't tool integration — it's the relational/judgment core.

title: Property appraisers and assessors
exposure_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing data, comparing market trends, and generating reports, all of which are highly susceptible to Automated Valuation Models (AVMs) and AI-driven data analysis. While physical site inspections and the need to defend assessments in public hearings provide a buffer, the shift toward 'desktop appraisals' and AI-assisted mass assessments significantly reduces the human labor required per valuation.
tier_rationale: Property appraisal involves substantial site inspection and physical observation, but the analytical/valuation core (comparables analysis, report writing, mass appraisal modeling) is knowledge work. An AI agent for this role needs integration with jurisdiction-specific assessment databases, MLS/comparable sales data, GIS systems, and locality-specific tax assessment rules, plus configuration to defend valuations under each jurisdiction's appeals process. The skills are similar across appraisers, but each enterprise/locality deployment requires meaningful integration and rule tuning, making it T2.

title: Public relations and fundraising managers
exposure_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.
tier_rationale: PR and fundraising management centers on relationships—with journalists, donors, executives, and community stakeholders—and on representing an organization's reputation in high-stakes, trust-dependent contexts. An AI agent can draft press releases, prepare grant applications, research donors, and analyze media trends, but the core value of in-person donor cultivation, media rapport, and crisis judgment stays with the human manager.

title: Public relations specialists
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—writing press releases, drafting speeches, creating social media content, and monitoring public sentiment—are digital-first and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level strategy and real-time interpersonal crisis management provide some protection, the vast majority of the daily work product can be generated or significantly optimized by AI, leading to high exposure and potential workforce consolidation.
tier_rationale: PR work depends heavily on relationships with specific journalists, media contacts, and executive stakeholders, plus deep knowledge of the client's voice, ongoing narrative, and sensitive internal context. An agent can draft press releases, monitor social sentiment, and prepare briefing materials, but the relational core—pitching reporters, managing crisis communications, and speaking on behalf of executives—remains with the human. While some tacit brand/voice knowledge could be documented (T3a-like), the persistent value lies in trusted relationships.

title: Public safety telecommunicators
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of this role—receiving digital inputs (voice, text, video), processing information according to protocols, and dispatching resources—are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the high-stakes nature and need for human empathy provide a temporary buffer, AI's ability to transcribe, translate, and prioritize emergency calls in real-time will significantly restructure the occupation and increase individual worker productivity.
tier_rationale: A 911 dispatcher AI agent requires deep per-PSAP integration: computer-aided dispatch (CAD) systems, local geography/GIS, agency-specific protocols, radio channel management, and connections to local police/fire/EMS units. The skill of triaging emergency calls is similar across jurisdictions, but each deployment needs custom integrations and protocol tuning, plus heavy compliance/escalation logic — classic T2 shape.

title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
exposure_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing price proposals, monitoring inventory, and reviewing financial reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While high-stakes negotiations and physical site visits provide a buffer, the majority of the work is digital information processing that can be significantly streamlined by AI, leading to higher productivity per worker and a restructuring of the procurement process.
tier_rationale: A procurement agent needs deep integration into the enterprise's ERP/procurement systems (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Oracle), supplier databases, contract repositories, and inventory systems, plus configuration around the company's approval workflows, vendor relationships, and category-specific policies. The skills are similar across companies, but each deployment requires substantial per-enterprise integration and tuning. Supplier relationships add some T3c flavor for senior roles, but the bulk of buyer/agent work is system-mediated transactional procurement.

title: Receptionists
exposure_rationale: Many core tasks such as scheduling, answering phones, and data entry are highly susceptible to automation via AI voice agents and chatbots. While the role retains a physical component for greeting and security, the BLS already projects zero growth due to automation, and the digital nature of the administrative workload makes it highly exposed to further restructuring.
tier_rationale: A receptionist agent would need to integrate with each enterprise's specific phone system, calendar/scheduling software, visitor management, EHR or CRM, and be tuned to the organization's escalation rules and information disclosure policies. The skills are generic but the deployment requires per-customer integrations and configuration (similar to customer service agents). Physical greeting is partially being replaced by kiosks/digital check-in, which fits the T2 deployment pattern.

title: Sales engineers
exposure_rationale: Sales engineers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including technical presentations, product customization, and troubleshooting. While AI can automate technical documentation, demo generation, and data-driven customer insights, the role's heavy reliance on complex interpersonal relationships, negotiation, and trust-building provides a significant buffer against full automation.
tier_rationale: Sales engineers combine technical product expertise with relationship-driven selling — their value lies in established customer trust, tailoring solutions to specific buyer contexts, and negotiating deals. An AI agent can assist with technical demos, RFP responses, and product configuration, but the customer relationship, in-person presentations, and negotiation must remain with the human. The relational core makes this T3c.

title: Sales managers
exposure_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.
tier_rationale: Sales managers' work centers on leading, motivating, and developing sales teams, plus maintaining relationships with key customers, distributors, and cross-functional stakeholders. While analytics, forecasting, and pipeline review can be automated with CRM-integrated agents (T2-like), the core leadership and relational components — coaching reps, closing escalated deals, internal political navigation — stay with the human.

title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
exposure_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—scheduling, drafting correspondence, managing databases, and transcribing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of LLMs and AI agents. While some roles require physical presence for visitor greeting or office management, the vast majority of tasks are routine information processing that can be automated or significantly streamlined, leading to major restructuring and reduced headcount per organization.
tier_rationale: An AI agent for this role needs deep integration with the enterprise's calendar, email, CRM, document management, and (for medical/legal subspecialties) EHR or case management systems, plus tuning to the specific executive's or office's preferences and workflows. The skills are generic across companies but deployment requires substantial per-enterprise configuration of integrations and escalation logic, mirroring the customer service agent pattern.

title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
exposure_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
tier_rationale: The core value of brokers, investment bankers, and financial sales agents lies in client relationships, trust, and book-of-business — they cold-call, network, and maintain long-term advisory ties with individuals and corporate executives. An AI agent can augment portable parts (market analysis, deal modeling, pitch deck drafting, compliance checks) but cannot replace the relational core required to win and retain clients in high-stakes financial transactions. This places the role firmly in T3c.

title: Set and exhibit designers
exposure_rationale: The core creative process—including script analysis, conceptual sketching, and CAD modeling—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI and automated design tools that can rapidly produce 3D environments and visual concepts. While the role requires physical-world considerations like material budgets and on-site installation oversight, the shift toward digital production (such as virtual sets and LED volumes) and AI-assisted drafting significantly increases productivity and reduces the total human labor required per project.
tier_rationale: Set and exhibit designers use standard professional tools (CAD programs, sketching software) to produce designs based on scripts, briefs, and project parameters. An AI agent could perform much of the design work using these same standard tools without requiring enterprise-specific integration—each project is essentially self-contained with portable inputs and outputs. The work is largely freelance/per-project, reinforcing that no deep enterprise customization is needed.

title: Sociologists
exposure_rationale: Sociology is a knowledge-intensive field where core tasks like data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. While qualitative field research and interpersonal interviews provide a buffer, the shift toward digital data collection and AI-driven statistical modeling significantly increases worker productivity and reshapes the research process.
tier_rationale: Sociological research uses standard methods (surveys, interviews, statistical analysis) and standard tools (SPSS, R, Stata, qualitative coding software) that are consistent across employers. An AI agent could conduct literature reviews, design studies, analyze data, and draft reports using these generic tools without per-enterprise integration. The work product (papers, reports, policy analyses) is portable across institutions.

title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
exposure_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and test automation being primary use cases for Large Language Models. While high-level system architecture and complex stakeholder communication provide some insulation, AI is drastically increasing individual productivity, which will likely lead to significant restructuring of entry-level roles and QA functions.
tier_rationale: Software development uses standard tools (git, IDEs, file system) that horizontal coding agents like Claude Code can access at any company without per-enterprise integration. The codebase itself provides the context; no months-long configuration is needed to deploy the agent at a new company.

title: Special effects artists and animators
exposure_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, with core tasks like 3D modeling, rendering, and animation being directly disrupted by generative AI and automated rigging tools. While high-level creative direction and complex team collaboration remain human-centric, AI is rapidly automating the 'routine' aspects of CGI and visual effects, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of workers needed for large-scale productions.
tier_rationale: Special effects artists and animators use standard professional tools (Maya, Blender, After Effects, Houdini, Unreal) that are common across studios, with project files accessed through standard file systems. An AI agent could be built once to operate these tools and produce assets at any studio without per-enterprise integration. While studios have proprietary pipelines, the customization is minimal — analogous to a software developer's codebase access.

title: Survey researchers
exposure_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
tier_rationale: Survey research uses standard tools (statistical software like SPSS/R/Stata, survey platforms like Qualtrics) and produces standard outputs (survey designs, statistical analyses, reports) that are similar across employers. A horizontal AI agent with access to these tools and the project files could design surveys, run statistical analyses, and write up results without substantial per-enterprise integration. The work resembles a generic data analyst more than a deeply embedded enterprise role.

title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
exposure_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—reviewing financial documents, identifying discrepancies, and interpreting tax law—are digital information-processing tasks where AI excels. While revenue agents handling complex corporate audits and collectors performing field investigations require human judgment and interpersonal skills, a significant portion of the routine examination and data verification work is already being automated or enhanced by sophisticated algorithms.
tier_rationale: An AI agent for this role would need deep integration with government tax systems (IRS IMF/BMF, state revenue databases, case management systems), access to taxpayer records, and configuration tuned to specific agency enforcement procedures and legal compliance requirements. The skills (reviewing returns, applying tax code, collections workflows) are similar across agencies, but deploying an agent requires substantial per-agency integration into proprietary tax infrastructure with strict compliance/audit controls. This matches the T2 pattern of a common product framework with heavy enterprise-specific configuration.

title: Technical writers
exposure_rationale: Technical writing is a fully digital occupation centered on synthesizing complex information into clear documentation, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already draft manuals, summarize technical specs, and generate code documentation, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human writers needed for routine documentation tasks.
tier_rationale: Technical writing requires deep familiarity with a specific company's products, internal terminology, style guides, and source materials (engineer interviews, product specs, code). An agent needs access to this proprietary knowledge and documentation systems, but it's documentable through artifact analysis and structured extraction from SMEs, after which the work becomes a T2-style deployment with integrations into the company's docs platforms and product repositories.

title: Training and development managers
exposure_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.
tier_rationale: Training and development managers' core work involves aligning training with organizational goals, conferring with department heads and executives to identify needs, managing staff, and overseeing budgets — work that depends on internal relationships, political context, and stakeholder buy-in specific to the organization. An agent could draft training materials, evaluate vendors, and analyze effectiveness, but the relational and leadership core (negotiating priorities with executives, motivating staff, building cross-functional consensus) stays with the human. Tacit organizational knowledge could partly be extracted, but the persistent value lives in ongoing relationships with leaders and subject-matter experts.

title: Training and development specialists
exposure_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
tier_rationale: Training specialists must assess organization-specific needs, design materials tailored to internal processes/policies, and deliver instruction tied to a company's culture and systems. Much of this tacit context (workflows, compliance requirements, role-specific skills) can be extracted via interviews and artifact analysis, then converted into a T2-style configured agent. Pure horizontal authoring tools aren't enough because the content is deeply enterprise-specific.

title: Travel agents
exposure_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.
tier_rationale: Travel agent work uses standard industry reservation systems (GDS like Sabre/Amadeus), supplier portals, and CRM systems that require per-agency integration and configuration. An AI agent would need connectors to these booking systems, agency-specific preferred-supplier rules, commission structures, and client profile databases, plus tuned escalation logic for travel disruptions. The skills are generic but enterprise deployment requires substantial integration work.

title: Tutors
exposure_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
tier_rationale: Tutoring uses standard educational content (curricula, practice problems, standardized test materials) that is consistent across students and institutions. A horizontal AI agent like ChatGPT/Khanmigo can deliver tutoring directly to students without per-enterprise integration — the 'customization' is just adapting to the individual student's level. No deep institutional system integration is required.

title: Urban and regional planners
exposure_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
tier_rationale: Urban planning's core value involves stakeholder relationships—with public officials, developers, community groups, and residents—through meetings, hearings, and negotiated consensus-building over local political dynamics. An AI agent could handle portable tasks like GIS analysis, zoning research, data gathering, and drafting reports, but the relational and political work of building stakeholder trust and navigating community politics stays with the human planner. While there's also tacit local knowledge (T3a-like), the relational/political core of the role is the binding constraint.

title: Web developers and digital designers
exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks like writing code (HTML, JavaScript), designing layouts, and creating prototypes being areas where AI has demonstrated high proficiency. AI tools can now generate functional code from natural language and create sophisticated visual mockups, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of developers needed for standard projects.
tier_rationale: Web development and digital design use standard tools (VS Code, Figma, browsers, git) across companies, and a horizontal AI agent like Claude Code or a design copilot can do the work by accessing the project's codebase and design files without per-enterprise integration. The work is genericizable — skills and outputs (HTML/CSS/JS, mockups) are standardized industry-wide.

title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
exposure_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
tier_rationale: B2B sales value lives in established buyer relationships, trust, and long-cycle account management with specific customers. An agent can handle lead research, prospecting outreach, quote prep, CRM updates, and follow-ups, but the relational core—negotiating, building rapport, closing—stays with the human rep.

title: Writers and authors
exposure_rationale: The core work product of writers and authors is entirely digital and text-based, which aligns perfectly with the primary capabilities of Large Language Models. AI can now generate drafts, conduct research, and perform editing tasks across various formats like blogs, copy, and scripts, significantly increasing productivity while simultaneously threatening to automate many entry-level and routine writing roles.
tier_rationale: Writing is a generic knowledge task with standard inputs (briefs, research) and outputs (prose) using standard tools (word processors, CMSs). A horizontal LLM-based agent can perform this work at any organization without per-enterprise integration — just provide the brief and style guide.
